Source URL: https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Find_Your_SBDC.asp

# Find Your Local SBDC by ZIP or County

## Resource type
Locator / local-office routing resource.

## Best for
Entrepreneurs who need to identify the correct SBDC office based on ZIP, county, or service area.

## Best-fit BOSS lifecycle stages
All stages.

## Use when the entrepreneur says
"Which SBDC should I contact?" "I am in 77084." "I am in Houston/Katy/Fort Bend/Harris/Waller."

## Resource summary
The Find Your SBDC page allows users to identify the nearest SBDC and references no-cost advising and training by ZIP/county/service area.

## What the user can do with this resource
Enter ZIP or select county/service area; use the result for local office routing.

## BOSS routing notes
This should be the first local-office routing source for ZIP-sensitive recommendations.

## Related source URLs
- https://www.sba.gov/local-assistance/find?type=26&zip=77084

## BOSS Library use notes
- Treat this as a resource-routing document, not as legal, tax, lending, certification, or procurement advice.
- Use the linked source URL as the user-facing official link.
- Ask discovery questions before routing when the user is early-stage or unclear.
- For Houston/77084 users, verify county/service-area fit through the official SBDC/SBA locator before giving a final local-office referral.
